videos.description:

videos.title: Casey Neistat's guide to posting every day | The Vergecast
videos.description:
The Vergecast is officially a daily show! We kick things off with the return of 90 Seconds on The Verge, a peek at the top stories on theverge.com. Then, we turn to our old pal Casey Neistat for some advice. Casey vlogged every day for 800 days straight, and has some thoughts on the pros and cons of daily posting, the state of YouTube in 2026, and how to make things every day without losing your mind in the process.

0:00 Intro - The Vergecast goes daily
1:10 90 Seconds on The Verge
2:44 Welcome

videos.title: Nvidia announces the RTX Spark
videos.description:
Nvidia is putting a complete computing chip — not just graphics — into the very heart of laptops and mini-PCs. After many months of leaks, it’s finally announcing the RTX Spark, the first in a family of chips that will meet or beat the most powerful thin-and-light Windows machines ever, it claims. Almost every major laptop vendor is accounted for, with eight specific laptops already confirmed for this fall like ASUS, Dell, and Microsoft.

Nvidia claims this adds up to “a new personal computing p

videos.title: Steam Controller plays music without speakers
videos.description:
You may have heard that Valve’s new Steam Controller can ring like a telephone or make the Wilhelm Scream and it can do it all without a speaker. But did you know it can sing songs, too? We played a few to hear some classics through the controllers haptic vibrations.

videos.title: Ever wonder what it'd look like if the Olympics allowed steroids?
videos.description:
Ever wonder what it'd look like if the Olympics allowed steroids? The Enhanced Games tried to answer that question with a competition between athletes fueled with varying degrees of peptides, supplements, HGH, Adderall, etc. The Verge's, Victoria Song, went to the Enhanced Games in Las Vegas to see the juiced-up action. Photos by Anthony Louis

videos.title: Jony Ive's funky Ferrari | The Vergecast
videos.description: The Ferrari Luce is here, and suffice to say it is not the electric Ferrari anyone expected. Nilay and David dig into the Jony Ive-designed car, from its marvelously appointed interior to its decidedly non-Ferrari-like exterior. (You might even call it... Nissan Leaf-like.) After that, the hosts discuss some of the latest backlash against AI, Google's ongoing AI-based changes to Search, and AI content labels. videos.title: Sony enters the RGB LED backlighting space with its new TVs
videos.description:
Sony enters the RGB LED backlighting space with its new TVs, the Bravia 7 II, and the flagship Bravia 9 II. RGB TVs like the Bravia 7 II use red, green, and blue LEDs instead of a field of all-blue or white LEDs for the backlight. This allows for an RGB LED TV to display more, and brighter, colors without as much reliance on its color filter. The picture is beautiful but the price is less appealing. Read our full review for the Bravia 7 II at TheVerge.com.
